steps.title: Apply the Zero Product Property
steps.content:
When a product of factors equals zero, at least one factor must equal zero. Set each factor equal to zero:
$$x - 18 = 0$$
$$x - 10 = 0$$
$$x + 3 = 0$$
$$x + 19 = 0$$

steps.title: Solve each factor to find all solutions
steps.content:
Solving for $x$ in each equation:
  • $x - 18 = 0 \Rightarrow x = 18$
  • $x - 10 = 0 \Rightarrow x = 10$
  • $x + 3 = 0 \Rightarrow x = -3$
  • $x + 19 = 0 \Rightarrow x = -19$
Among all solutions, the positive values are 18 and 10.
